story.text: As bdo, I need a proposal-only PR to land on main as a record without ceremony — the same door reports and done-lines already pass through — so the blueprints my arcs owe before building ('blueprint before build', #348) stop stranding off-trunk on the proposal-landing friction (#355), because I said 'land blueprints as proposal-records' (2026-06-23).

briefing.headline: The proposals records door — a blueprint lands as a record, no atom needed
briefing.value: A proposal is design captured ahead of a build — the bundle a non-trivial arc owes first. Today it has no atom to carry (the build comes later), so a proposal-only PR reads as an off-log orphan and strands off main, exactly the proposal-landing friction #355 names. This widens the records door (done-line 0172) to add .ai-native/proposals/ alongside reports/ and done/: a proposal-only branch is backed without an atom, the same shape as the phrasing and records doors.
briefing.why_now: bdo said 'land blueprints as proposal-records' (2026-06-23) and 'blueprint before build' makes a proposal first-class progress (#348/CTA-3). With blueprints required before building but unable to reach main on their own, the foundation work the owner steers from accretes off-trunk where he cannot see it. One line in RECORD_DIRS fixes it.
briefing.if_accepted: A proposal-only PR lands as a record through the same off-log gate that already passes reports and done-lines; blueprints reach the surface bdo steers from instead of stranding on dozens of off-trunk branches.
briefing.if_rejected: Proposals stay off-log orphans: a blueprint-only PR is refused at the gate and strands off main, and 'blueprint before build' keeps producing work that cannot land until a build atom is attached after the fact.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: the change is one entry in RECORD_DIRS and a docstring; the door's teeth are unchanged (a single code/atom/log/config change still closes it, so a proposal cannot smuggle work), proven by the existing and added §10 tests. Reverting is removing one tuple entry.
briefing.mechanism: loop/pr_audit.py adds '.ai-native/proposals/' to RECORD_DIRS, so records_only() (and through it orphan_reason's THIRD backed shape) treats a proposals-only .md branch as records-only. The reach (pr.py audit) recomputes records_only from the diff, so the door cannot be lied to. tests/test_pr_audit.py adds a case proving a proposals-only branch is records-only and a case proving a proposal + a .py file is NOT (the teeth stay).
